entries.title: Initialize Hermes Orchestrator and complete WIP UI branch directly
entries.resolution: Orchestrator initialized; first UI MVP implementation pass completed with TypeScript/build validation. Remaining backend/liveness validation stays in backlog.

history.note: Initial project-owner feedback received via Telegram.
Feedback changes orchestration/design scope and requires backlog creation.
Design docs and implementation work started.
First direct implementation cycle completed and reviewed.

entries.title: PDF parser cannot extract text from compressed streams (FlateDecode)
entries.resolution: Added FlateDecode (zlib/deflate) decompression to extractStreams in internal/parser/pdf.go. Stream dictionaries are inspected for /Filter /FlateDecode and decompressed before text extraction. Verified with fpdf2-generated PDF: previously returned 'no extractable text found', now extracts 47 paragraphs across 4 pages. All existing parser tests pass plus new FlateDecode-specific test. Backend container rebuilt and deployed.

history.note: User reported PDF upload fails with 'no extractable text found'. fpdf2-generated PDF uses FlateDecode compression which the parser does not handle.
High priority — PDF is the primary upload format. Adding FlateDecode decompression to extractStreams.

Implementation complete. FlateDecode decompression added to extractStreams, tested with fpdf2 PDF (47 paragraphs extracted), all tests pass, container rebuilt and deployed. PDF upload confirmed working via frontend proxy.

entries.title: Delete Books
entries.resolution: Implemented Delete Book actions for both /player three-dot menu and Continue Listening. Backend DELETE /api/v1/books/{bookId} removes stored book artifacts; frontend confirms deletion, clears playback state when needed, invalidates book queries, and navigates away from the deleted player. Verified by containerized Go tests and web build.

history.note: Manual Hermes Orchestrator cycle started after stale triggered supervisor failed; backlog item and OpenCode worker run created.
Implementation completed via OpenCode DeepSeek R4 Pro worker, supervisor reconciled fixes, Go tests and web build passed.

entries.title: Persist reusable test voice samples
entries.resolution: Implemented filesystem-backed reusable voice preview samples under storage path voice-samples/. Backend pre-generates missing samples after startup/TTS voice listing, PreviewVoice serves cached samples instead of synthesizing per click, samples persist in the existing backend data volume across restarts, and responses include cached=true when served from storage.

history.note: User requested Voices tab preview playback to use generated/stored test samples instead of synthesizing every click.
Implemented directly with TDD in the Hermes Orchestrator-enabled project.
Containerized Go tests, TypeScript check, web export, Docker rebuild, production backend restart, cache hit, and restart persistence checks passed.
